input.instructions: Design an efficient algorithm to insert all N bitstrings into a Trie. For each node in the Trie, track whether it represents the end of an original ID and the number of original IDs that pass through or end at this node. The total number of nodes can be up to 10^6, so use an adjacency list or array-based Trie to avoid recursion depth issues in Python.

Solve the question directly.

Keep decision_notes extremely short. Prefer 3 to 8 words.

Keep the justification to 12 words or fewer.
Use plain text only inside the response field. Do not use markdown formatting, code fences, or math-style backslashes such as \equiv.
Do not add any extra text before or after the JSON object.

}

Question:
Design an efficient algorithm to insert all N bitstrings into a Trie. For each node in the Trie, track whether it represents the end of an original ID and the number of original IDs that pass through or end at this node. The total number of nodes can be up to 10^6, so use an adjacency list or array-based Trie to avoid recursion depth issues in Python.

results.content:
FINAL_ANSWER: Use an array-based Trie with child pointers, end-of-ID flags, and pass-through counts.
JUSTIFICATION: Array-based implementation avoids recursion and manages large node counts efficiently.
